title: RV8GR Lab 13 marker trace
purpose: Package the Lab 13 full-system pass program through the $AA marker so LI, ADDI, SUBI, taken BEQ, bus ownership, and final pass state are checked before physical single-step.

chips.role: ROM DBUS to IBUS fetch bridge
LI, ADDI, SUBI, AC, and Z-flag execute path
BEQ uses Z to load PC with {PG,IRL}
T2 immediate operand driver onto IBUS

wiring.rule: T0/T1 fetch uses ROM as the only DBUS source.
Fetch phases use U7; T2 immediate execute uses U34.
Rises during T2 for LI, ADDI, and SUBI in this program.
LOW only for the taken BEQ at clock 12; fail-path J at $0008 is not reached before the $AA marker.
SUBI $15 must set Z=1 before BEQ $0C executes.

behavior.program: ROM bytes $0000-$000F are the Lab 13 Verify ALL paths program. This package traces through clock 15, where AC becomes $AA.
behavior.marker_flow: LI $10 makes AC=$10, ADDI $05 makes AC=$15, SUBI $15 makes AC=$00 and Z=1, BEQ $0C skips the fail path, and LI $AA writes the pass marker.
behavior.bus_policy: T0/T1 fetch rows have U7 as the only IBUS driver and ROM1 as the only DBUS driver. T2 immediate rows have U34 as the only IBUS driver.
behavior.final_pass_state: After 15 clean manual clock edges, PC=$000E, AC=$AA, Z=0, PG=$00, and DP=$80.

timing.clocking: 15 manual clock edges: five 2-byte instructions with T0/T1/T2 phases.
timing.frequency_boundary: Functional and manual single-step proof only; physical 1 MHz or 5 MHz claims require timing_margins.json evidence.
timing.deadband_boundary: The marker trace still needs R/C settling, output-disable deadband, and destination setup/hold evidence before physical speed signoff.
timing.student_rule: Check the bus owners and the branch at clock 12 before trusting the $AA LEDs.

guarantees: Lab 13 ROM bytes match the source pass program through $000F
Z is set by SUBI $15 and cleared by LI $AA
BEQ $0C is taken and the fail-path J at $0008 is skipped
Every row has single-driver IBUS and DBUS ownership
The package does not promote a physical high-speed claim

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
